100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 21, 1926, the British Empire faced mounting tensions as the General Strike loomed over labor disputes, drawing attention to the struggles of workers across the nation. In the United States, President Calvin Coolidge addressed the nation, emphasizing the importance of economic stability amid rising concerns over inflation. Meanwhile, in Europe, Italy's Mussolini continued to consolidate power, stirring debates about fascism's impact on the continent.
